rationale: Both notes center on SEC FRR44 and firms’ advertising-spending disclosure behavior: the citing study examines post-FRR44 voluntary disclosure decisions and peer effects, while the cited study builds and validates an FRR44 disclosure-based firm-strategy indicator. That overlap makes the cited paper a plausible prior methodological/foundational source in the same disclosure domain, not an obviously incidental mention, so it should be retained for full relation judgment.
